title: Unit Fixtures, Properties, Fuzzing, Race Detection, and Leak Evidence · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed operations review reconstructs unit fixtures, properties, fuzzing, race detection, and leak evidence from revision, tenant, source URL, request validators, response bytes, parsed identity, transaction, lease, state transition, output, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for layered deterministic Go evidence suite after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging…
objectives: Create small original RSS, Atom, namespace, encoding, date, identity, update, malformed, entity, oversized, and extension fixtures with explicit expected evidence.
Test URL, discovery, parse, normalize, schedule, state, cursor, search, and policy functions with injected clocks and deterministic inputs.
Fuzz XML tokens, URLs, dates, cursors, state transitions, and normalization with bounded invariants and preserved minimal failures.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A review Go team handles case 3322 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile original fixture coverage, pure units, fuzz properties, race and leak cases, and evidence non-claims; every goroutine, body, row set, transaction, lease, timer, and pool needs named cleanup ownership; then change one tenant, cursor, reader-state, authorization, cache, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Create small original RSS, Atom, namespace, encoding, date, identity, update, malformed, entity, oversized, and extension fixtures with explicit expected evidence.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, import one convenient trusted RSS feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, XML and HTTP budgets, PostgreSQL constraints and transaction, schedule, lease, goroutine cleanup, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as feed-service proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Feed Go Test Nonclaims, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
